paradigm: beat 'em up / platformer with non-linear mansion exploration

danger_signals.visual_form: Digitized grotesque enemy sprites throughout mansion rooms
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat; signals unsafe areas

danger_signals.visual_form: Time limit counter for each level (on-screen timer)
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger state; failure to complete objectives within time results in character death or permanent loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Large grotesque creature visually distinct from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Boss-tier threat; narrative danger to family members (Jennifer kidnapping)

danger_signals.visual_form: Final boss entity with supernatural visual design
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate world threat; represents corrupting supernatural force targeting David's psychic abilities

danger_signals.visual_form: Mansion rooms displaying unnatural, warped environmental aesthetics
danger_signals.meaning: World-state degradation; ancient evil has infiltrated safe domestic space

safety_signals.visual_form: Baseball bats, cleavers, and other melee implements scattered in rooms
safety_signals.meaning: Resources for player empowerment; signals player agency and readiness to confront threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Extra lives and health restoration items placed throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe resource availability; world still provides survival support

safety_signals.visual_form: In-game map displayed after clearing enemy rooms, showing multiple route choices
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation control and player agency restored; safe moment to plan next action

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual confirmation when all monsters defeated in a space
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety state; allows brief respite before moving forward

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rick's mutated form when Terror Mask power activated (hulking, grotesque, chest-flesh weapon extension)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mansion spaces; domestic Connecticut setting represents Rick's claimed normalcy and Wall Street success

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: The Dark Stone (power object), Terror Mask (corrupting artifact)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple monster types with grotesque digitized designs; boreworm and final boss forms
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mansion corruption, unnatural room aesthetics, monster spawning points
Kidnapping locations within mansion; Jennifer as plot objective in level 2; David as final objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mansion represents wealth and domestic success (Wall Street prosperity, Connecticut estate)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rick believes he has moved past Terror Mask trauma; family unit intact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Monster infestation throughout mansion rooms; warped environmental aesthetics; Eldritch energy present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Monsters actively patrol/attack; Terror Mask reawakens and begins communicating with Rick; family members kidnapped

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Introduction of boss entities (Boreworm, Evil One); Dark Stone presence; reality distortion signals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Evil One revealed as orchestrator; revealed plan to exploit David's latent psychic abilities; time pressure intensifies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Terror Mask's true nature revealed through dialogue; visual climax with Dark Stone challenge
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rick must destroy the Mask; final transformation battle mechanics activated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mask shatters; sky clears in good ending; family reunion visual or loss imagery in bad endings
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ending varies: 'Good Ending' shows family reunification with pleasant tone; bad/partial endings show grief, loss, solitude

environmental_storytelling.example: Mansion rooms become increasingly warped and dangerous as player progresses; aesthetic decay mirrors narrative threat escalation
Failing to reach Jennifer before time expires results in her death/consumption by boreworm; mechanical failure becomes story consequence
Weapons found in specific rooms signal safe resource points; their presence indicates player has breathing room to prepare
Map only appears after clearing enemy rooms, signaling enemies defeated and player can strategize next approach
Mansion (symbol of Rick's success and family sanctuary) invaded by monsters; visual communication that no safe haven exists
Jennifer and David's presence/absence communicated through level objectives and ending variations; their fate shapes narrative outcome

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Engage Rick on sight; attack patterns indicate active threat state
Boss encounter at level 2 end; must be defeated within time limit or Jennifer dies; demonstrates time pressure lethality
Uses Jennifer as distraction while taking David; final boss battle; reveals plan to weaponize David's psychic abilities
Communicates with Rick throughout game; initially seems helpful (grants power-up transformation); reveals manipulative true intent at game end
Standard combat speed; weapon-based attacks; vulnerable to enemy damage

entity_behavioral_tells.state: With mask power active (Eldritch Orbs collected)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hulking grotesque appearance; significantly increased strength; flesh-weapon special attack with extended range; throws weapons for higher damage

notes: Splatterhouse 3 communicates world-state primarily through time-pressure mechanics tied to narrative consequences and environmental corruption visuals. The game employs non-linear mansion exploration with map-based choice to signal player agency during safe moments, contrasting with monster-filled rooms representing active threat zones. The Terror Mask functions as a visual/mechanical corruption symbol—offering power while concealing manipulative intent. Multiple endings (4 total) directly tied to player performance (time management and objective completion) create dynamic world-state outcomes. The shift from side-scrolling linearity to mansion exploration mirrors Rick's shift from outsider-victim to protagonist defending domestic space. Digitized sprite graphics and grotesque enemy designs communicate danger through visual grotesqueness rather than color-coded systems.
